title: Emiliano Martínez completes glove training session in Argentina World Cup boost - ESPN
original_text:
Emiliano Martínez completes glove training session in Argentina World Cup boost
play
Alejandro Moreno discusses Argentina's World Cup chances as they will face Austria, Algeria and Jordan in Group J. (1:08)
Moreno: Austria could be 'problematic' for Argentina in Group J (1:08)
Adriana Garcia
Jun 12, 2026, 09:35 AM
Argentina goalkeeper Emiliano 'Dibu' Martínez has completed his first full glove session after recovering from a finger injury.
The Aston Villa star fractured a bone in the ring finger of his right hand on May 20 during the warm-up before his team's Europa League final win against Freiburg.
The veteran goalkeeper wore a splint during the initial recovery phase and trained with one glove.
He returned to train on Thursday with both gloves.
Martínez missed the warm-up wins against Honduras and Iceland but is expected to start in Argentina's World Cup opener against Algeria on June 16 in Kansas City.
Martínez, 33, is coming off a strong season with Villa, who finished fourth in the Premier League.
- How to watch 2026 WC opening ceremonies
- England's WC prep: Tuchel's squad survive storms
- Are Scotland this World Cup's dark horses?
He was named in the Europa League Team of the Season.
A 2022 World Cup winner with Argentina, Martínez was voted the best goalkeeper.
He was also awarded the Golden Glove as he helped his country win the 2024 Copa América.

2026 World Cup updates: USMNT in control against Paraguay
ESPN
Jun 13, 2026, 02:39 AM
The 2026 FIFA World Cup is fully underway and now it's time for the United States to kick off its tournament, as the Americans take on Paraguay at Los Angeles Stadium (SoFi Stadium).
Can Mauricio Pochettino's team begin on the right foot or will La Albirroja spoil the party? Join us for all the updates from everything going on in California from our experts and contributors.
